Steps: Open the RetailLite app on Android. On the login screen, tap the 'Sign in with Google' button. A native Android account picker dialog appears — select your Google account and confirm.
Expected Result: The app authenticates via Google OAuth, creates/fetches the user session, and navigates to the main dashboard. Shop name and data are loaded from Firestore.
Automation: ❌ Manual — DEVICE REQUIRED (Android): Install app, tap Google Sign-In, verify native account picker shows accounts on device, select account, verify login and dashboard load

Steps: Open the RetailLite web app in a desktop browser. Click 'Sign in with Google'. A browser popup window opens showing the Google account chooser — select your account.
Expected Result: The popup closes, the user is authenticated, and the web dashboard loads with shop data.
Automation: ❌ Manual — BROWSER REQUIRED (Web): Open app in Chrome, tap Google Sign-In, verify OAuth popup opens, complete Google login, verify redirect and dashboard load

Steps: Open the RetailLite Windows desktop app. Click 'Sign in with Google'. A browser window opens for Google OAuth. Complete the sign-in in the browser.
Expected Result: The browser redirects to the desktop bridge URL, the app receives the OAuth code, completes authentication, and lands on the dashboard.
Automation: ❌ Manual — BROWSER REQUIRED (Windows): Open app on Windows, tap Google Sign-In, verify default browser opens OAuth URL, complete Google login, verify app session active

Steps: Open the POS (Billing) screen. Tap the barcode/camera icon in the product search area. The camera viewfinder opens. Point the camera at a product's barcode.
Expected Result: The barcode is scanned, the product is looked up by its barcode field, and it is automatically added to the cart with quantity 1. A torch toggle is available in the scanner.
Code Status: ✅ Implemented (BarcodeScannerService + mobile_scanner bottom sheet + torch toggle)
Automation: ❌ Manual — HARDWARE REQUIRED (Android camera): Open POS, tap barcode scanner icon, point camera at real barcode, verify product matched and added to cart automatically

Test Name: Instant UPI app open on Android
Steps: Send a UPI payment link to a customer via WhatsApp. Open the link on an Android phone by tapping it in WhatsApp.
Expected Result: Android immediately shows the UPI app chooser (GPay, PhonePe, Paytm, etc.) without loading any web page. The merchant's UPI ID is pre-filled.
Automation: ❌ Manual — DEVICE REQUIRED (Android with UPI app installed): Open customer payment page, tap 'Pay Now', verify system redirects to installed UPI app (PhonePe/GPay/Paytm)

Steps: Generate a payment link for ₹500 (am=500 in the URL). Open this link on an Android phone.
Expected Result: The UPI app chooser opens and the selected UPI app shows ₹500 pre-filled in the amount field along with the payee name.
Automation: ❌ Manual — DEVICE REQUIRED (Android with UPI app): Complete UPI payment, return to app, verify payment confirmation screen shown and transaction recorded in khata

Steps: Open the payment page on a desktop. Use a phone's UPI app to scan the QR code shown on the screen.
Expected Result: The UPI app on the phone reads the QR correctly and pre-fills the payment information — correct merchant UPI ID, amount, and payee name. Payment can proceed normally.
Automation: ❌ Manual — DEVICE REQUIRED (Second Android device with UPI app): Scan QR code shown on POS screen using another device's UPI app camera; verify correct amount pre-filled

Steps: Open a payment link on Android. Instead of using the UPI chooser, tap the 'Pay Now' button on the page.
Expected Result: url_launcher opens the UPI deep-link, bringing up the UPI app chooser so the user can complete the payment.
Automation: ❌ Manual — DEVICE REQUIRED (Android): Test with no UPI app installed; tap 'Pay Now', verify fallback dialog appears with UPI ID text and copy button

Steps: On a Windows device, go to Settings → Hardware → USB Printer tab. Tap the Refresh button.
Expected Result: The app queries the Windows print spooler and lists all installed printers. Each printer name appears in the list.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HARDWARE REQUIRED (Windows PC with printers installed): Open Hardware Settings > USB tab on Windows, verify installed system printer names appear in dropdown list

Steps: On the USB printer list (Windows), tap on a printer from the list.
Expected Result: The printer is selected and a green checkmark appears next to it. The selected printer name is stored as the default USB printer.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HARDWARE REQUIRED (Windows with printer): Select printer from USB list, verify app stores selection; on next bill print, correct printer is pre-selected

Steps: With a USB printer selected on Windows, tap 'Test Print'.
Expected Result: The test page job is sent via Windows RAW printing to the selected printer. The printer outputs a test page.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HARDWARE REQUIRED (Windows USB/network printer): Tap Test Print for USB printer, verify physical receipt printed with test content on selected printer

Steps: With USB printer selected on Windows, complete a bill from POS.
Expected Result: The receipt is sent to the USB printer using Windows RAW mode. The printer outputs the formatted receipt with all bill details.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HARDWARE REQUIRED (Windows USB printer): Complete sale in POS on Windows, verify receipt sent to selected USB printer and prints successfully

Steps: Go to Settings → Hardware → Bluetooth tab on Android. Tap the Scan button.
Expected Result: The device scans for nearby Bluetooth devices. Thermal printers in range (like Xprinter or GoojPrint) appear in the list with their device names and MAC addresses.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HARDWARE REQUIRED (Bluetooth thermal printer in BT range): Open Hardware Settings > Bluetooth tab, tap Scan, verify nearby BT printer appears in device list

Steps: From the Bluetooth device list, tap a printer to connect.
Expected Result: The app pairs and connects via Bluetooth to the thermal printer. The status indicator turns green. The connected printer name is shown.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HARDWARE REQUIRED (Bluetooth printer in range): Select BT printer from list, tap Pair & Connect, verify pairing completes and status shows Connected

Steps: With Bluetooth printer connected, tap Test Print.
Expected Result: An ESC/POS formatted test page is sent via Bluetooth. The thermal printer outputs the test receipt with paper size and font configuration details.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HARDWARE REQUIRED (BT printer connected): Tap Test Print for BT printer, verify BT printer advances paper and prints test receipt correctly

Steps: With Bluetooth printer connected on Android, complete a bill from POS.
Expected Result: The ESC/POS receipt bytes are sent via Bluetooth. The thermal printer outputs the full receipt: shop name, items, total, payment method, and footer.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HARDWARE REQUIRED (BT printer connected, Android): Complete bill in POS, verify receipt auto-prints on Bluetooth printer if auto-print enabled

Steps: Log into the same account on two devices (Device A and Device B). Change the theme colour on Device A.
Expected Result: Within a short time, Device B reflects the same theme colour change — synchronized via ThemeSettingsNotifier → OfflineStorageService → Firestore Cloud sync.
Code Status: ✅ Implemented (ThemeSettingsNotifier syncs theme via OfflineStorageService to Firestore; SyncSettingsService manages sync intervals)
Automation: ❌ Manual — TWO DEVICES REQUIRED (same account): Change a setting on Device A, within 30 seconds open Settings on Device B, verify same value has synced via Firestore

Steps: On the Windows app, trigger a low stock notification (complete a bill that drops stock below threshold) while the app is running.
Expected Result: A Windows toast notification appears in the bottom-right corner of the screen with the low stock message.
Automation: ❌ Manual — DEVICE REQUIRED (Windows): Trigger a notification (e.g. low stock alert) on Windows; verify Windows native toast notification appears in system notification area

Steps: On Android, have a newer version available on the Play Store. Open the app.
Expected Result: The in-app update prompt appears — either a gentle banner (flexible update) or a full-screen overlay (immediate/forced update) depending on the version difference.
Automation: ❌ Manual — DEVICE REQUIRED (Android, Play Store version): With a newer version available in Play Store, open app; verify in-app update dialog appears with Update Now button

Steps: From the update banner or update dialog, click 'Download Update'.
Expected Result: The MSIX installer is downloaded in the background. A progress indicator may be shown. When the download completes, the user is prompted to install. After install, the app relaunches at the new version.
Code Status: ✅ Implemented (WindowsUpdateService 5-layer: silent download → watchdog → retry x3 → UpdateDialog)
Automation: ❌ Manual — WINDOWS DEVICE REQUIRED: Tap 'Download and Install' in update banner; verify MSIX file downloads, Windows installer launches, app restarts with new version

Steps: Ensure an optional update is available via Remote Config. Open the Windows app and leave it running for 30+ seconds without clicking anything.
Expected Result: The installer is downloaded silently in the background with no UI interruption. Download completes quietly so the user can install at their convenience via the update dialog.
Automation: ❌ Manual — WINDOWS DEVICE REQUIRED: Leave update available; verify background download starts silently; on next app launch verify 'Install now' prompt shows already-downloaded file

Steps: In the admin panel, navigate to Errors.
Expected Result: A list of recent error reports from the in-app Firestore error logging system (ErrorLoggingService.getGroupedErrors) is shown with error message, stack trace snippet, affected version, and platform.
Automation: ❌ Manual — FIREBASE CONSOLE REQUIRED: Trigger a real app error, open Super Admin > Errors screen, verify error entries visible with stack trace and grouped error details

Test Name: Broadcast push notification to all users
Steps: In the admin Notifications section, compose a broadcast title and body message. Tap 'Send to All'.
Expected Result: An FCM push notification is sent to all users with a registered FCM token. It appears on all user devices (Android/Web) with the composed title and message.
Automation: ❌ Manual — FIREBASE + REAL DEVICES REQUIRED: Send broadcast notification from Admin screen, verify FCM message received on at least one real device with correct title/body

Steps: On the Windows development machine, open a terminal in the project root. Run `./smart-deploy.ps1`. Answer the interactive prompts (version number, which platforms to deploy). Wait for completion.
Expected Result: The script builds and deploys all selected platforms successfully. Web is hosted to Firebase Hosting; Android APK/AAB is built; Windows MSIX is packaged. Each step shows a success confirmation.
Automation: ❌ Manual — CI/CD: Run smart-deploy.ps1 full cycle; verify Android APK/AAB built, web deployed to Firebase Hosting, version JSON updated, deploy-backups entry created

Steps: After a web deploy, open https://stores.tulasierp.com/app in a browser.
Expected Result: The latest app version loads. The version number in Settings matches the deployed build. No errors in the browser console from missing assets.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HOSTING: After web deploy, open https://[your-hosting-url] in browser; verify app loads, no 404 on routes, Firebase Hosting cache correct

Steps: Open https://stores.tulasierp.com in a browser.
Expected Result: The marketing landing page loads with the correct app name, features list, download links, and call-to-action buttons.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HOSTING: Open marketing website URL; verify homepage, pricing, and download sections load correctly with no broken links or images

Steps: Open https://stores.tulasierp.com/pay?pa=testshop@okaxis&am=100 in a browser.
Expected Result: The UPI payment page loads. On mobile, the UPI app chooser is triggered. On desktop, a QR code for ₹100 payment to testshop@okaxis is displayed.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HOSTING: Open /payment route on hosted web; verify UPI payment page loads with correct shop info and QR code when tested with valid URL params

Steps: Open the download page of the app site.
Expected Result: Correct version numbers are shown next to the Android APK and Windows MSIX download links, matching the latest deployed build versions.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HOSTING: Open /download route; verify Windows and Android download buttons have current version numbers matching latest deploy-backup JSON

Steps: Deploy a new version of the web app. Then open the web app in Chrome with a hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R).
Expected Result: The new version loads without any stale cached assets. The service worker cache is cleared properly on deploy.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HOSTING: After deploy, hard-refresh browser (Ctrl+Shift+R), check Network tab; verify service worker fetches new assets and old cached version not used

Steps: Add a product to inventory and set its Barcode field to a known barcode (e.g. 8901030861924). Go to POS and use the barcode scanner. Scan that barcode.
Expected Result: The product is found by its barcode field and added to the cart immediately.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HARDWARE REQUIRED (Android camera + physical barcode): Scan a barcode on a real product; verify product appears in search results and correct details shown

Steps: In POS, scan a barcode that does not match any existing product in inventory.
Expected Result: A prompt appears offering to add a new product with the scanned barcode pre-filled in the barcode field of AddProductModal. The cashier can quickly add it.
Automation: ❌ Manual — HARDWARE REQUIRED (Android camera + unknown barcode): Scan barcode not in product database; verify 'Product not found' dialog with option to add new product

Steps: Use the app continuously for 30+ minutes — create bills, browse products, check Khata, and navigate between all screens.
Expected Result: The app remains smooth and responsive throughout. No memory-related slowdowns, frame drops, or crashes. Navigation returns to correct states.
Automation: ❌ Manual — PERFORMANCE (Android or Windows device): Use app continuously for 30 minutes — browse reports, add bills, navigate; verify no crash, no memory warning, no slowdown

Steps: Log into the same account simultaneously on two Android devices (phone + tablet). On Device A, add a new product.
Expected Result: Within a few seconds, the new product appears in the Products list on Device B — pushed via Firestore's realtime listener.
Code Status: ✅ Implemented (Firestore realtime listeners in all providers)
Automation: ❌ Manual — TWO DEVICES REQUIRED: Log into same account on two devices simultaneously; verify both show same shop data and both remain logged in without session conflict

Steps: On two devices, open the same product's detail screen simultaneously. Edit the price on both devices at the same time (with slight delay), then save both.
Expected Result: Both saves succeed without crashing. The final value persists as whatever was saved last (last-write-wins by Firestore server timestamp). No data corruption or sync error occurs.
Automation: ❌ Manual — TWO DEVICES REQUIRED: Edit the same product name from both devices within 5 seconds; verify Firestore last-write-wins and both devices eventually show same value

Steps: Log into the same account on a phone and a tablet. Create a bill on the phone.
Expected Result: Within seconds the bill appears in the Bills History screen on the tablet, pushed by Firestore's realtime stream listener.
Automation: ❌ Manual — TWO DEVICES REQUIRED: Create a bill on Device A; within 15 seconds open Bills list on Device B; verify the new bill appears without requiring a manual refresh

Steps: On Android, enable TalkBack in Accessibility settings. Open the app and navigate through the POS screen — tap buttons and form fields.
Expected Result: TalkBack reads out the label of each button and field as it is focused. Interactive elements have meaningful accessibility labels (e.g. 'Search products', 'Add to cart', 'Checkout').
Automation: ❌ Manual — DEVICE REQUIRED (Android with TalkBack): Enable TalkBack in Android Accessibility Settings; navigate app; verify all buttons read meaningful labels, no silent touchable areas

Steps: Fresh install the app → open app → check debug logs/console for Firebase initialization messages
Expected Result: Firebase Auth, FCM, Analytics, Crashlytics, AppCheck, RemoteConfig all initialize without error. Dashboard loads successfully.
Automation: ❌ Manual — DEVICE REQUIRED: Fresh install app on real device; open and verify all Firebase services init (check Crashlytics dashboard, Analytics events, FCM token in logs)

Steps: Set maintenanceMode=true in Firebase Remote Config → force fetch → open app
Expected Result: Maintenance screen shown with message, no access to any features. Dismissing/back does not bypass.
Automation: ❌ Manual — FIREBASE CONSOLE REQUIRED: Set maintenanceMode=true in Remote Config; open app; verify maintenance screen blocks all access

Steps: Set minimumVersion to a version higher than current app version in Remote Config → open app
Expected Result: Force update screen shown with link to Play Store/download. User cannot bypass.
Automation: ❌ Manual — FIREBASE CONSOLE REQUIRED: Set minimumVersion higher than current in Remote Config; open app; verify force update screen shows with correct store link

Steps: Time from splash screen appearance to dashboard ready on cold start (4G network, mid-range Android device)
Expected Result: All Firebase services initialize in parallel. Dashboard loads in under 3 seconds on 4G.
Automation: ❌ Manual — DEVICE + STOPWATCH: Fresh launch app on mid-range Android with 4G; time splash to dashboard; verify < 3 seconds

Steps: Use app for 1+ days with data retention enabled in settings. Check Firestore for old temporary data.
Expected Result: Old temporary data (draft bills, expired caches) cleaned up per retention policy without user action.
Automation: ❌ Manual — MULTI-DAY TEST: Use app normally for 2+ days; verify temporary/draft data older than retention period is automatically cleaned

Steps: On Windows with a newer version available in Remote Config → open app
Expected Result: Update banner appears at top of screen. 'Download Update' button opens correct download URL.
Automation: ❌ Manual — WINDOWS REQUIRED: Set newer version in Remote Config; open Windows app; verify update banner shows with working download link

Steps: Set announcement text in Firebase Remote Config → force fetch → open app
Expected Result: Announcement banner shows at top with correct text. Can be dismissed. Does not reappear after dismiss until next announcement.
Automation: ❌ Manual — FIREBASE CONSOLE REQUIRED: Set announcement text in Remote Config; open app; verify banner shows; dismiss it; verify it stays dismissed

Steps: Open web app in Chrome → go offline (DevTools Network → Offline) → create a bill → go back online
Expected Result: Bill is created locally via IndexedDB. When connection restored, bill syncs to Firestore within seconds.
Automation: ❌ Manual — BROWSER REQUIRED (Chrome): Open web app; go offline in DevTools; create bill; go online; verify bill appears in Firestore

Steps: Load web app with large dataset (100+ products, 500+ bills). Check IndexedDB size in Chrome DevTools → Application → Storage.
Expected Result: IndexedDB cache stays within reasonable bounds. App does not crash or slow down significantly.
Automation: ❌ Manual — BROWSER REQUIRED: Load app with large dataset; check IndexedDB in DevTools; verify reasonable size and no performance degradation

Steps: Open web app → create products and bills → hard refresh browser (Ctrl+Shift+R)
Expected Result: Data still available after hard refresh. Products and bills load from IndexedDB cache before Firestore fetch completes.
Automation: ❌ Manual — BROWSER REQUIRED: Create data in web app; hard refresh; verify data loads instantly from cache before network fetch

Steps: Open web app → go offline → navigate between screens
Expected Result: Static assets (images, fonts, icons) load from service worker cache. Screens that don't need network data render correctly.
Automation: ❌ Manual — BROWSER REQUIRED: Open web app; go offline in DevTools; navigate between screens; verify static assets load from SW cache
